paper_evaluations.title: Safety and effectiveness of surgical fixation versus non-surgical methods for the treatment of flail chest in adult populations: a systematic review and meta-analysis.

20d.reason: No sensitivity analyses results are presented.

21.reason: No assessment of bias due to missing results is reported.

comparison_details.title: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis Comparing the Safety and Efficacy of Spinal Anesthesia and Spinal Anesthesia Combined with Obturator Nerve Block in Transurethral Resection of Bladder Tumors.

items.ai_reason: Title explicitly includes “Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis”.
Introduction explains clinical problem and need for evaluating ONB with spinal anesthesia.
Objective to evaluate safety and efficacy of SA vs SA+ONB is clearly stated.
Inclusion and exclusion criteria are detailed in Section 2.1.
All searched databases and date range (Jan 2000–Dec 2021) are listed in Section 2.2.
Only a brief keyword string is given; full search strategies for each database are absent.
Two reviewers screened records independently with a third reviewer for disputes (Section 2.3).
Data extraction performed by two independent reviewers; conversion procedures described (Section 2.4).
Primary outcomes (bladder perforation, obturator reflex, stay, recurrence) are listed.
Other variables (author, year, age, gender, intervention details) are enumerated in data extraction.
Risk of bias assessed with Cochrane tool and Jadad scale (Section 2.5).
Effect measures (RR for dichotomous, MD for continuous) specified in Statistical Methods.
No explicit description of how studies were grouped for each synthesis beyond inclusion criteria.
Data conversion (e.g., hospital stay units) is described; handling of missing data not detailed but some preparation methods are given.
Use of forest plots, funnel plots, and tables for visual/tabular presentation is reported.
Synthesis methods, models, heterogeneity assessment, and software (R) are fully described (Section 2.6).
Subgroup analysis by ONB approach and heterogeneity investigation are reported.
Sensitivity analysis by omitting each study is described (Section 2.6).
Publication bias assessed with funnel plot; method for detecting missing-result bias is provided.
No method (e.g., GRADE) for assessing certainty of evidence is reported.
Search and selection numbers are presented with a flow diagram (Figure 1).
Excluded studies are not listed nor reasons for exclusion given.

No certainty/ confidence (e.g., GRADE) assessments are provided.
